organizations.notes: Sandy (Sanford) Dochen, longtime Austin civic leader; UT Austin grad, 21-year IBM career as corporate social responsibility/citizenship director (retired 2019), now affiliated with Amplify Credit Union. 78731 (Northwest Hills) matches. Sits on numerous boards. Gave to both Brigid Shea and Ann Howard.

organizations.role: Board Member; National Jewish Democratic Council secretary
organizations.notes: CEO Go! Retail/Calendar Club; documented ADL Austin involvement; also formerly secretary Elie Wiesel Foundation for Humanity
CURRENT ADL board member (10+ years). Armbrust and Brown attorney (Austin commercial real estate law firm). Land use / business litigation practice.

organizations.role: Past Board Chair (2009-2012); helped create Austin Hate Crimes Task Force; expanded No Place for Hate program
organizations.notes: Wikipedia biography of Steve Adler (58th Austin mayor 2015-2023) explicitly documents he "served as the board chair of the Anti-Defamation League Austin Region" from 2009 to 2012 and notes ADL board-chair work alongside Texas Tribune/Ballet Austin civic roles. Disambiguation: Fuentes donor listed as "mayor" at Austin 78701 matches Steve Adler, former Austin mayor.
Helped establish the Austin ADL chapter. Per PolitiFact 2014: led board 2009-2012. Per Forward: "He made ADL the go-to civil rights organization in Austin" — Kirk Rudy quote.

Texas Book Festival board bio explicitly identifies Marc Winkelman as "secretary of the National Jewish Democratic Council". NJDC is a Democratic-aligned Jewish pro-Israel political organization. Disambiguation: Fuentes Winkelman, Marc / calendar executive services / Austin 78744 = Marc Winkelman, CEO Calendar Club / Calendar Holdings (Austin HQ).

organizations.org: HillCo Partners (on behalf of Intercontinental Terminals Company, LLC)
organizations.role: Co-Founder / Registered Texas Lobbyist for O&G midstream-storage client
organizations.notes: F Minus database (sourced from Texas Ethics Commission filings) lists William J. Miller at HillCo Partners as registered TX lobbyist for Intercontinental Terminals Company, LLC (ITC) — Mitsui-owned bulk petroleum/petrochemical storage terminals at Deer Park & Pasadena TX, 19M barrels capacity, serving "global petrochemical and petroleum markets" (fuel oil, bunker oil, distillates, BTX, methanol). Midstream fossil-fuel storage infrastructure — directly analogous to the Enterprise Products / Targa midstream clients flagged in Velasquez file. Miller is HillCo co-founder (1998, with Buddy Jones); donor address Austin 78703 matches HillCo HQ.

organizations.role: team member / advocate (oil & gas regulatory accountability)
organizations.notes: Ira John Yates of Paonia, CO 81428 (ZIP matches) is a descendant of rancher/oil pioneer Ira Griffith Yates Jr. and an active mineral-interest owner in the historic Yates Oil Field; he is affiliated with Commission Shift, an oil-and-gas regulatory-accountability advocacy group (orphaned wells, riverbed lease cleanup). Distinctive name + Paonia location + oil heritage corroborate; gave $3,000 to Shea and Howard 2022-2024.

organizations.org: Yates Oil Field (Yates family mineral interests, Pecos County TX)
organizations.role: mineral-interest owner / heir of Ira G. Yates Jr.
organizations.notes: Ira John Yates of Paonia, CO 81428 (ZIP matches) is a descendant of rancher/oil pioneer Ira Griffith Yates Jr. and an active mineral-interest owner in the historic Yates Oil Field; he is affiliated with Commission Shift, an oil-and-gas regulatory-accountability advocacy group (orphaned wells, riverbed lease cleanup). Distinctive name + Paonia location + oil heritage corroborate; gave $3,000 to Shea and Howard 2022-2024.

A Mary Beth Maher of Austin, TX holds mineral rights/royalty interests (6 interests across 2 counties per mineralholders.com), matching name+city (78703). This confirms she owns Texas mineral/royalty rights but not an occupation/career; cannot confidently assign an industry. Strong Dem FEC lean (0.94); gave to Brigid Shea and Ann Howard.
